description: Build executable object-level scene layouts before simulation, including support surfaces, non-overlap placement, scale, orientation, camera coverage, and physics graph membership.
physical_assumptions: A physical simulation starts from a valid static scene state.
Physics-critical objects require stable ids, roles, transforms, colliders, mass/material metadata, and collision profiles.
Initial overlap and unsupported placement should be caught before runtime.
Camera and lighting plans are evidence requirements, not decorative UI settings.
verifier_rules: Objects have unique ids and roles.
Physics-critical assets have collider, mass/material, and collision profile.
Initial transforms avoid invalid overlap unless the case is a negative overlap test.
Required camera views cover the event region.
repair_suggestions: Bind missing physics metadata or mark an analytic proxy.
Adjust object positions, scales, and support surfaces.
Regenerate camera plan around the event bounds.
